divergence: The sun was privatized and daylight became a premium tier
story_angle: investigative report whose author is promoted mid-article specifically to stop the investigation
headline: Daylight Rationing Sparks Outrage as Aristocrats Bloom in Unfettered Sunlight
deck: As the Imperial Sunlight & Gas Syndicate celebrates ten years of profitable dusk, residents of East End suffer a pall over their morning tea.
article:
The Imperial Sunlight & Gas Syndicate diverted 2,184 premium solar minutes from Bethnal Green to Mayfair orchid houses on the morning the district awoke under fog.

Mr. Thaddeus Quill's reporting on the diversion earned him an immediate post as Director Quill, Office of Tier Compliance, complete with gas-scrip salary and a brass plaque from Chairman Sir Reginald P. Foggbottom.

Foggbottom toasted the Syndicate's tenth anniversary while restating its duty to premium subscribers; the Grand Illumination Gala will demonstrate metered daylight to invited guests.

East End households now conduct their afternoons by gaslight, their petitions for dawn answered with additional dusk.
author: Mr. Thaddeus Quill, Special Correspondent

oped.title: A Modest Defence of Measured Mornings: Why Grumbling Over Gas Serves None
oped.author: Viscount Archibald Fumblecrump, Esq., Perpetual Subscriber to Tier III Illumination and Self-Appointed Keeper of the Syndicate's Ledger
oped.body: The recent caterwauling from East End over daylight rationing betrays a lamentable want of perspective. The Imperial Sunlight & Gas Syndicate has, these ten profitable years, spared us the vulgar excess of unfiltered dawn, which any gentleman knows breeds freckles and revolutionary thoughts. Those complaining souls might simply purchase the Premium Solar Voucher instead of squandering coin on inferior tea leaves. Their hats, after all, are far too wide; narrower brims would admit what scant rays they deserve. I myself enjoy uninterrupted beams above my conservatory, as is only fitting for one who advised the original privatisation bill. If anything, the remedy lies in further restriction: let the districts without subscriptions receive only the moon's pallid charity, thereby encouraging thrift and discouraging idleness. Envy, not scarcity, darkens their mornings.

classifieds.text: Owing to the Syndicate's most profitable dusk, a respectable widow of Stepney must part with her last two hours of morning light, transferable by voucher; enquiries to the rear of the Black Swan public house, terms desperate.

East End household requires a nimble lad to climb gas standards at dawn and beg stray photons from passing carriages; board of stale bread and threepence nightly for those who return with light enough to read the tea leaves.

Lost upon the 12th instant: one allotted quarter-hour of sunlight, mislaid between the counting-house and the fog; finder returning same to Mr. Cratchit of Houndsditch shall receive a half-pint of darkness in exchange.

The Imperial Sunlight & Gas Syndicate regrets to inform its East End subscribers that any further petitions for dawn shall be met with an increase in dusk; all complaints to be submitted in writing and by gaslight only.

panels.caption: East Ender squints at the manor: "Their roses are bursting while my tea's gone black under the syndicate dusk."
Syndicate clerk adjusts the meter: "Premium daylight is reserved for those whose complexions can still turn a profit."
Child tugs sleeve: "If we buy an extra hour, can we bury Mum before the bill arrives?"

joke.setup: The Sunlight Allocation Board has ruled that only titled gentlemen may enjoy the full arc of the day without interruption.
joke.punchline: The rest of England is therefore encouraged to black their boots by the glow of their own class resentment.
joke.text: The Sunlight Allocation Board has ruled that only titled gentlemen may enjoy the full arc of the day without interruption. The rest of England is therefore encouraged to black their boots by the glow of their own class resentment.

sponsor_ads.headline: Surplus Morning Rays: Let by Affluent Households
sponsor_ads.body: Gentlemen of means having secured their premium allotments now sublet excess dawn hours by the quarter-minute; arrive pale, depart faintly gilded.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Payment in sovereigns or unrationed moonlight accepted.

sponsor_ads.headline: Dr. Sallow's Shade-Proof Tonic
sponsor_ads.body: For those condemned to the cheaper gloaming, this sovereign elixir permits the cultivation of a respectable complexion without so much as a single purchased lumen.
sponsor_ads.tagline: May cause spontaneous night-blindness; not for aristocrats.

sponsor_ads.headline: Public Caution from the Imperial Solarium Trust
sponsor_ads.body: Any person found attempting to harvest unauthorised daylight through mirrors, buckets, or hopeful expressions will forfeit their remaining ration and be fined one hour of perpetual dusk.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Report suspicious blooming to the nearest meter reader.

sponsor_ads.headline: Eclipse Silk for the Rationed Masses
sponsor_ads.body: Drape your windows and your hopes in our patented blackout fabric; now woven to withstand both envy and the occasional aristocratic glare.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Guaranteed to keep you suitably obscure until further notice.

real_world_echo: Mirrors US 250th birthday fireworks and extreme weather by framing the Syndicate's tenth-anniversary 'Grand Illumination Gala' as a lavish display of metered daylight bursts that leaves common districts in sudden, engineered dusk
